revision.text: {{Infobox Wikipedia user script |name=AutoSectionLink |desc=Adds or refines the /* section link */ as you edit so only the modified section will be linked in the summary |browsers=Chrome, Firefox, etc. |source=[[User:Nardog/AutoSectionLink.js]] }} This script adds or substitutes an accurate /* section link */ (which appears as →section link) in the summary as you edit a page (or every time you hit "Show changes" when editing an old revision). You have to have live preview enabled ({{myprefs|Editing|check={{int:tog-uselivepreview}}}}), or it won't work well. That is, it checks what section the modified parts of the source belong to and updates the summary accordingly. If no section heading precedes the first modified line, or the modified parts belong to no one particular section (i.e. the source has no headings or multiple highest-level sections have been modified), it adds no section link (and removes any existing one). Whenever the summary is changed by the script, an undo button appears next to the summary box. Clicking it will restore the previous section link. ==Installation== Add the following to [[Special:MyPage/common.js|your common.js]] or [[meta:Special:MyPage/global.js|global.js]]: mw.loader.load('//en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=User:Nardog/AutoSectionLink.js&action=raw&ctype=text/javascript'); Or you may enable the [[User:Enterprisey/script-installer|script installer]] in {{myprefs|Gadgets}} and click "Install" in the infobox on this page. ==Limitations== *Template transclusions in headings remain unparsed. This echoes the default behavior of section editing. *VisualEditor and the [[mw:2017 wikitext editor|2017 wikitext editor]] are not supported. *If you have live preview disabled, it won't work well because every time you show a preview or diff the script will think the new version of the text is the original. ==See also== *[[phab:T22307]] {{User:Nardog/scripts}}

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was 1 revision imported from [[:wikipedia:User:Nardog/AutoSectionLink]]
revision.text: {{Infobox Wikipedia user script |name=AutoSectionLink |desc=Adds or refines the /* section link */ as you edit so only the modified section will be linked in the summary |browsers=Chrome, Firefox, etc. |source=[[User:Nardog/AutoSectionLink.js]] }} This script adds or substitutes an accurate /* section link */ (which appears as →section link) in the summary as you edit a page (or every time you hit "Show changes" when editing an old revision). You have to have live preview enabled ({{myprefs|Editing|check={{int:tog-uselivepreview}}}}), or it won't work well. That is, it checks what section the modified parts of the source belong to and updates the summary accordingly. If no section heading precedes the first modified line, or the modified parts belong to no one particular section (i.e. the source has no headings or multiple highest-level sections have been modified), it adds no section link (and removes any existing one). Whenever the summary is changed by the script, an undo button appears next to the summary box. Clicking it will restore the previous section link. ==Installation== Add the following to [[Special:MyPage/common.js|your common.js]] or [[meta:Special:MyPage/global.js|global.js]]: mw.loader.load('//en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=User:Nardog/AutoSectionLink.js&action=raw&ctype=text/javascript'); Or you may enable the [[User:Enterprisey/script-installer|script installer]] in {{myprefs|Gadgets}} and click "Install" in the infobox on this page. ==Limitations== *Template transclusions in headings remain unparsed. This echoes the default behavior of section editing. *VisualEditor and the [[mw:2017 wikitext editor|2017 wikitext editor]] are not supported. *If you have live preview disabled, it won't work well because every time you show a preview or diff the script will think the new version of the text is the original. ==See also== *[[phab:T22307]] {{User:Nardog/scripts}}

{{Infobox Wikipedia user script |name=AutoSectionLink |desc=Adds or refines the /* section link */ as you edit so only the modified section will be linked in the summary |browsers=Chrome, Firefox, etc. |source=[[User:Nardog/AutoSectionLink.js]] }} This script adds or substitutes an accurate /* section link */ (which appears as →section link) in the summary as you edit a page (or every time you hit "Show changes" when editing an old revision). You have to have live preview enabled ({{myprefs|Editing|check={{int:tog-uselivepreview}}}}), or it won't work well. That is, it checks what section the modified parts of the source belong to and updates the summary accordingly. If no section heading precedes the first modified line, or the modified parts belong to no one particular section (i.e. the source has no headings or multiple highest-level sections have been modified), it adds no section link (and removes any existing one). Whenever the summary is changed by the script, an undo button appears next to the summary box. Clicking it will restore the previous section link. ==Installation== Add the following to [[Special:MyPage/common.js|your common.js]] or [[meta:Special:MyPage/global.js|global.js]]: mw.loader.load('//en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=User:Nardog/AutoSectionLink.js&action=raw&ctype=text/javascript'); Or you may enable the [[User:Enterprisey/script-installer|script installer]] in {{myprefs|Gadgets}} and click "Install" in the infobox on this page. ==Limitations== *Template transclusions in headings remain unparsed. This echoes the default behavior of section editing. *VisualEditor and the [[mw:2017 wikitext editor|2017 wikitext editor]] are not supported. *If you have live preview disabled, it won't work well because every time you show a preview or diff the script will think the new version of the text is the original. ==See also== *[[phab:T22307]] {{User:Nardog/scripts}}[[Category:reimport]]

{{Infobox Wikipedia user script |name=AutoSectionLink |desc=Adds or refines the /* section link */ as you edit so only the modified section will be linked in the summary |browsers=Chrome, Firefox, etc. |source=[[User:Nardog/AutoSectionLink.js]] }} This script adds or substitutes an accurate /* section link */ (which appears as →section link) in the summary as you edit a page (or every time you hit "Show changes" when editing an old revision). You have to have live preview enabled ({{myprefs|Editing|check={{int:tog-uselivepreview}}}}), or it won't work well. That is, it checks what section the modified parts of the source belong to and updates the summary accordingly. If no section heading precedes the first modified line, or the modified parts belong to no one particular section (i.e. the source has no headings or multiple highest-level sections have been modified), it adds no section link (and removes any existing one). Whenever the summary is changed by the script, an undo button appears next to the summary box. Clicking it will restore the previous section link. ==Installation== Add the following to [[Special:MyPage/common.js|your common.js]] or [[meta:Special:MyPage/global.js|global.js]]: mw.loader.load('//en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=User:Nardog/AutoSectionLink.js&action=raw&ctype=text/javascript'); Or you may enable the [[User:Enterprisey/script-installer|script installer]] in {{myprefs|Gadgets}} and click "Install" in the infobox on this page. ==Limitations== *Template transclusions in headings remain unparsed. This echoes the default behavior of section editing. *VisualEditor and the [[mw:2017 wikitext editor|2017 wikitext editor]] are not supported. *If you have live preview disabled, it won't work well because every time you show a preview or diff the script will think the new version of the text is the original. ==See also== *[[phab:T22307]] {{User:Nardog/scripts}}

{{Infobox Wikipedia user script |name=AutoSectionLink |desc=Adds or refines the /* section link */ as you edit so only the modified section will be linked in the summary |browsers=Chrome, Firefox, etc. |source=[[User:Nardog/AutoSectionLink.js]] }} This script adds or substitutes an accurate /* section link */ (which appears as →section link) in the summary as you edit a page (or every time you hit "Show changes" when editing an old revision). You have to have live preview enabled ({{myprefs|Editing|check={{int:tog-uselivepreview}}}}), or it won't work well. That is, it checks what section the modified parts of the source belong to and updates the summary accordingly. If no section heading precedes the first modified line, or the modified parts belong to no one particular section (i.e. the source has no headings or multiple highest-level sections have been modified), it adds no section link (and removes any existing one). Whenever the summary is changed by the script, an undo button appears next to the summary box. Clicking it will restore the previous section link. ==Installation== Add the following to [[Special:MyPage/common.js|your common.js]] or [[meta:Special:MyPage/global.js|global.js]]: mw.loader.load('//en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=User:Nardog/AutoSectionLink.js&action=raw&ctype=text/javascript'); Or you may enable the [[User:Enterprisey/script-installer|script installer]] in {{myprefs|Gadgets}} and click "Install" in the infobox on this page. ==Limitations== *Template transclusions in headings remain unparsed. This echoes the default behavior of section editing. *VisualEditor and the [[mw:2017 wikitext editor|2017 wikitext editor]] are not supported. *If you have live preview disabled, it won't work well because every time you show a preview or diff the script will think the new version of the text is the original. ==See also== *[[phab:T22307]] {{User:Nardog/scripts}}

revision.comment: [[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] many contributors, see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Nardog%2FAutoSectionLink here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24796114356|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Infobox Wikipedia user script |name=AutoSectionLink |desc=Adds or refines the /* section link */ as you edit so only the modified section will be linked in the summary |browsers=Chrome, Firefox, etc. |source=[[User:Nardog/AutoSectionLink.js]] }} This script adds or substitutes an accurate /* section link */ (which appears as →section link) in the summary as you edit a page (or every time you hit "Show changes" when editing an old revision). You have to have live preview enabled ({{myprefs|Editing|check={{int:tog-uselivepreview}}}}), or it won't work well. That is, it checks what section the modified parts of the source belong to and updates the summary accordingly. If no section heading precedes the first modified line, or the modified parts belong to no one particular section (i.e. the source has no headings or multiple highest-level sections have been modified), it adds no section link (and removes any existing one). Whenever the summary is changed by the script, an undo button appears next to the summary box. Clicking it will restore the previous section link. ==Installation== Add the following to [[Special:MyPage/common.js|your common.js]] or [[meta:Special:MyPage/global.js|global.js]]: mw.loader.load('//en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=User:Nardog/AutoSectionLink.js&action=raw&ctype=text/javascript'); Or you may enable the [[User:Enterprisey/script-installer|script installer]] in {{myprefs|Gadgets}} and click "Install" in the infobox on this page. ==Limitations== *Template transclusions in headings remain unparsed. This echoes the default behavior of section editing. *VisualEditor and the [[mw:2017 wikitext editor|2017 wikitext editor]] are not supported. *If you have live preview disabled, it won't work well because every time you show a preview or diff the script will think the new version of the text is the original. ==See also== *[[phab:T22307]] {{User:Nardog/scripts}}
